claim_text: We evaluated several state-of-the-art DFDs used in natural image perceptual quality competitions on MR images from our reader study, taking inspiration from the NTIRE Perceptual IQ Assessment Challenges.33
manuscript_section: Methods / Reader-study DFD evaluation (sub-claim attribution to ref 33)

sub_claims.text: gu2022 is the organizers' report of an NTIRE Perceptual Image Quality Assessment Challenge that evaluated state-of-the-art deep/learned full-reference (and blind) image-quality methods (DFDs) on natural images.

evidence.snippet: "title": "NTIRE 2021 Challenge on Perceptual Image Quality Assessment"

"This paper reports on the NTIRE 2021 challenge on perceptual image quality assessment (IQA), held in conjunction with the New Trends in Image Restoration and Enhancement workshop (NTIRE) workshop at CVPR 2021. ... In comparison with previous IQA challenges, the training and testing datasets in this challenge include the outputs of perceptual image processing algorithms and the corresponding subjective scores. Thus they can be used to develop and evaluate IQA methods on GAN-based distortions. The challenge has 270 registered participants in total. In the final testing stage, 13 participating teams submitted their models and fact sheets. Almost all of them have achieved much better results than existing IQA methods, while the winning method can demonstrate state-of-the-art performance."

"The NTIRE 2021 challenge takes a step forward in benchmarking perceptual IQA -the task of predicting the perceptual quality of an image obtained by perceptualoriented algorithms. We employ a new dataset called PIPAL [20] as our training set, which contains 200 reference images, 29k distorted images and 1.13M human judgements."

evidence.section: The NTIRE Challenge on Perceptual IQA (content.txt L8)
evidence.snippet: "We host the NTIRE 2021 Perceptual Image Quality Assessment Challenge and the objective are three-fold: (1) to push developing state-of-the-art perceptual image quality assessment algorithms to deal with the novel GAN-based distortion types; (2) to compare different solutions and gain new insights; and (3) to promote a novel large perceptual IQA dataset (PIPAL [20, 19])."

"There are 13 teams participated in the testing phase of the challenge. Table 1 reports the main results and important information of these teams. We also select some existing representative IQA methods as our baseline. Specifically, We choose PSNR, SSIM [54] and FSIM [59] as representative traditional IQA methods, PI [7] and NIQE [35] as representative blind IQA methods, and LPIPS [61], DISTS [12], PieAPP [39] and SWD [19] as representative deeplearning based methods."

"As shown in Table 1, 11 of 13 participating teams achieve an SRCC score higher than 0.75 on PIPAL, which significantly surpasses the highest performance of existing algorithms (0.65). The champion team achieves an SRCC score of 0.799 and a PLCC score of 0.790, refreshing the state-of-the-art performance on PIPAL."

"We employ a subset of the PIPAL dataset as the training set and an extended version of the PIPAL dataset as the validation and the testing set. The PIPAL dataset includes both traditional distortion types, image restoration results, compression results, and novel GAN-based image processing outputs." — confirms the challenge data are natural-image (PIPAL) photographs, not MR images, matching the manuscript's framing of taking 'inspiration from' a *natural image* perceptual-quality competition.

sub_claims.paper_value: NTIRE 2021 Challenge on Perceptual Image Quality Assessment (CVPR 2021 NTIRE workshop); 270 registered participants; 13 teams in final testing; PIPAL natural-image dataset; baselines include PSNR, SSIM, FSIM, PI, NIQE, LPIPS, DISTS, PieAPP, SWD.
sub_claims.claim_value: NTIRE Perceptual IQ Assessment Challenges (plural) — manuscript ref 33 = gu2022 (single challenge report).
sub_claims.nuance: Substance fully supported: the source IS a primary NTIRE Perceptual IQA challenge organizers' report that evaluates SOTA deep IQA methods on natural images, exactly the inspiration the manuscript cites it for. Substitution-provenance note: the citekey is 'gu2022' but the ingested PDF is the NTIRE 2021 instance (per meta.json title/abstract); per the v1 substitution note in the dispatch, the bibliography entry under gu2022 points to the 2021 organizers' report. The manuscript's plural 'Challenges' framing remains consistent with the NTIRE annual series. paper_value vs claim_value mismatch reflects metadata (year/series-name), not a quantitative-fact drift, so the CONFIRMED verdict at the sub-claim level is preserved and the metadata mismatch is surfaced via overall_flag=MINOR + RECITE remediation.

remediation.suggested_edit: Reconcile the bibliography entry for citekey 'gu2022' with the actual cited paper. The ingested source is Gu et al., 'NTIRE 2021 Challenge on Perceptual Image Quality Assessment' (CVPR 2021 NTIRE workshop). Either (a) rename the citekey to 'gu2021' and update the bib entry's title/year/venue to match the 2021 challenge report, or (b) if the manuscript truly intended to cite the NTIRE 2022 challenge report, fetch that paper and re-ground. Manuscript prose ('NTIRE Perceptual IQ Assessment Challenges', plural) need not change — the series framing is accurate either way.

phrasings_tried: NTIRE / 'New Trends in Image Restoration and Enhancement' / NTIRE workshop at CVPR
perceptual IQA / perceptual image quality assessment / IQA methods / FR-IQA / NR-IQA
challenge / competition / participating teams / leaderboard / state-of-the-art
natural images / PIPAL dataset / GAN-based distortions / perceptual-oriented algorithms
deep IQA / deep-learning-based IQA / LPIPS / DISTS / PieAPP / SWD (DFD-equivalent methods evaluated in the challenge)

section_checklist.section: The NTIRE Challenge on Perceptual IQA (content.txt L8-L14)
sections/ directory listing (Challenge Methods, Challenge Results, Introduction, Related Work, The NTIRE Challenge on Perceptual IQA, plus 13 per-team section files)

attestation.indirect_attribution_check: No indirect-attribution issue. gu2022 IS the primary organizers' report of the NTIRE 2021 Perceptual IQA Challenge — it directly hosts and documents the challenge, dataset (PIPAL), evaluation protocol (SRCC + PLCC), participating teams' methods, and final results. The manuscript's reference 33 is correctly pointed at this primary source for 'NTIRE Perceptual IQ Assessment Challenges'.
attestation.out_of_context_check: No out-of-context issue for the citation function. The manuscript cites gu2022 as the inspiration source for an evaluation methodology (evaluating multiple state-of-the-art deep full-reference distortion/quality metrics — DFDs — against subjective reader-study scores). gu2022 does exactly this on natural images via the NTIRE 2021 challenge structure, so using it as 'inspiration' for an analogous MR-image evaluation is contextually appropriate. The only minor wrinkle is the year/series-name mismatch (manuscript says 'NTIRE Perceptual IQ Assessment Challenges' plural; fetched PDF is the NTIRE 2021 instance; the citekey is gu2022 — see substitution note in nuance and RECITE remediation).
